description: Hardcoded type-checking and dispatching for request body serialization.
reasoning: The method is responsible for handling various input types (data, files, json). To support a new serialization format (e.g., Protobuf or MessagePack) or a new input type, the method body must be modified with additional conditional logic rather than being extended via a registry or strategy pattern.

description: Monolithic method signature closed to extension for new request parameters.
reasoning: The request method explicitly lists every possible configuration parameter (auth, proxies, hooks, etc.). Adding any new cross-cutting feature to the library (like a new caching strategy or retry configuration) requires modifying the method signature and the internal call to PreparedRequest.prepare, violating the principle of being closed to modification.

description: Hardcoded logic for HTTP method transitions during redirects.
reasoning: This method contains the specific logic for how HTTP methods change (e.g., POST to GET) based on status codes (301, 302, etc.). To support a new redirect behavior or a non-standard status code transition, this method must be modified directly.

description: Hardcoded dependency on environment variables for proxy discovery.
reasoning: The function is closed to extension for other proxy discovery mechanisms (e.g., system registry on Windows, configuration files, or platform-specific APIs). Adding a new source for proxy settings requires modifying the logic within this utility function.
